observations: The grid shows all twelve masters above their silhouette triptychs at a consistent card scale, making the extreme body plans of Veyra, Creep, Malachor, Korath, and Nyxa immediately separable.

Cashmere, Quillith, Luna, and Sable share tall fit humanoid proportions, fitted dark garments, long legs, and a hand- or weapon-led pose; their distinguishing outer cues are respectively a folio, tablet, diagonal strands, and rifle.
The roster shares dark charcoal presentation backgrounds, low-saturation brown-black materials, centralized full-body framing, and high fine-detail density.
The board visibly labels one selected silhouette beneath every unit.
defects: Small master panels compress face and material differences, while selected-silhouette badges and metadata take part of each card's visual attention.
Several humanoid cards rely on internal props or thin lines rather than strongly different body outlines at this scale.
uncertainties: Because names, roles, traits, and selected variants are printed beside the images, this board cannot establish unaided unit identification or blind selection inference.
contamination: Answer-bearing unit names, roles, traits, and explicit SELECTED silhouette labels are displayed with the product images.

observations: The same-master crops show strong mechanical variety at the extremes: Korath's lowered human scowl, Veyra's split apertures, Nyxa's taut predatory snarl, Creep's blank eye cavities, and Malachor's exposed heart without a face.
Cashmere, Knoll, Quillith, Luna, and Sable each use a controlled asymmetric half-smile or lifted mouth corner with narrowed or sideways eyes, creating a visible expression-family cluster.
Kett uses direct compressed-brow confrontation, while Pilfer uses a larger sly grin with one eye hidden by hair.
Each crop is printed directly beside prose naming private want, primary fear, active survival strategy, current emotion, contradiction, visible face direction, and forbidden read.
defects: The intended psychology prose occupies more panel area than the face images and directs interpretation before a blind facial read can be recorded.
The non-faced Veyra and Malachor panels are presented under the same face-question format despite offering no conventional gaze or expression evidence.
uncertainties: No unaided psychology conclusion can be isolated from this board after the adjacent answer text is visible.
contamination: The board explicitly supplies the intended wants, fears, strategies, emotions, contradictions, facial interpretation, and forbidden readings for every unit.

observations: Across the twelve crops, facial mechanisms range from human eyes and mouths to one slit plus a toothed aperture, hollow mask eyes, and an exposed organ with no head.
Among readable humanoid faces, several rely on narrowed eyes and a restrained one-corner smile, while Korath, Nyxa, and Kett carry more compressed anger or predation.
The shared dark backgrounds, close three-quarter framing, fine skin markings, and brown-gray palette make material and lighting treatment broadly consistent across the board.
defects: The repeated half-smile family among multiple humanoids reduces emotional separation when viewed side by side.
Dense adjacent psychology prose prevents the face row from functioning as a clean image-only roster comparison.
uncertainties: Expression distinctiveness cannot be evaluated as an unaided roster read once the intended interpretation is printed beside every crop.
contamination: The roster comparison shares the same answer-bearing psychology text and demographic or role labels as E050.

observations: The twelve exact-size derivatives are displayed together without redrawing; Veyra's oval shell, Nyxa's bow, Creep's radial tendrils, Malachor's pincer, Korath's arch, Kett's fist, and Sable's rifle remain strong unit-scale cues.
Cashmere, Quillith, and Luna reduce to slim dark humanoids differentiated mainly by a rectangle, an extended hand, or one pale diagonal line.
Knoll's pale paper and Pilfer's amber claw cluster remain visible, but their finer mechanisms and expressions do not.
Every thumbnail is accompanied by the unit name, role, trait list, and primary trait label.
defects: The surrounding labels reveal identity and role before the thumbnail must carry that information unaided.
Several dark humanoid thumbnails merge clothing and limbs into narrow columns against their backgrounds.
uncertainties: The board cannot prove unaided name, role, or trait recognition because those answers are printed immediately beside each image.
contamination: Answer-bearing unit names, gameplay roles, trait lists, and PRIMARY trait labels are displayed beside every native-size image.

observations: At shared unit scale, the five nonstandard body plans and the arch-, bow-, tendril-, pincer-, fist-, and rifle-led silhouettes separate more readily than the tall dark humanoids.
The roster maintains one dark presentation environment and restrained palette, but that shared treatment compresses internal distinctions among Cashmere, Knoll, Quillith, Luna, and Sable.
Unique pale accents such as Knoll's paper, Luna's strand, and Sable's wrapped rifle become the principal separators for several humanoids.
defects: Text metadata dominates each card and makes the side-by-side visual read secondary.
Fine facial, supernatural-cost, and material cues are not recoverable from most thumbnails.
uncertainties: Clean image-only roster distinctiveness remains unmeasured by this annotated board.
contamination: The roster alias carries the same answer-bearing identity, role, and trait labels as E051.

observations: The role-grouped images make the three tank bodies visibly distinct: a humanoid ruin-bearer, sealed oval organism, and low pincer crawler.
The two mage images share a tall fit feminine body and open-hand staging, while the two supports share upright humanoid presentation plus one rectangular carried object.
The assassin pair and marksman pair are visibly dissimilar in body plan because each group combines one extreme creature silhouette with one humanoid.
Every card prints lane, prop or anatomy, visible cost, emotion, and contradiction directly below its master.
defects: Large blocks of intended design text prevent the image comparison from remaining blind and consume substantial area that could otherwise support larger side-by-side masters.
Some wide creatures are letterboxed while tall humanoids fill vertical frames, making detail scale unequal across role groups.
uncertainties: Visible role and psychology matching cannot be separated from the supplied role, lane, cost, emotion, and contradiction labels.
contamination: The board explicitly provides role groups and intended lane, dominant prop or anatomy, supernatural cost, emotion, and contradiction for each master.

observations: The top reference image shows a dark-robed woman surrounded by many curled paper strips, a long feathered weapon, blue filament effects, copper trim, layered cloth, and multiple distinct surface materials.

The twelve masters below share darker gray-brown backgrounds and generally narrower palettes; the creature designs separate through large body plans, while several humanoids repeat fitted dark leather-like garments, long straps or tabs, and one hand-led pose.
Cashmere, Knoll, Quillith, and Sable visibly echo the reference image's dark tailored clothing and paper, tablet, strip, or record motifs, though their props and body poses differ.
The comparison board prints a reference function, compare-first instruction, do-not-copy warning, phase question, source paths, hashes, and lane, emotion, and dominant-idea captions.
defects: The reference is displayed much larger than any individual Phase 2 master, so small-detail comparisons are not at equal image scale.
The reference's effects, papers, and costume occupy a broader palette and larger compositional field than the isolated Phase 2 masters, limiting direct like-for-like composition reading.
uncertainties: A clean blind style comparison cannot be recovered after the board explicitly names what to compare, what not to copy, and each unit's intended lane, emotion, and dominant idea.
contamination: The board explicitly identifies Vellum as the benchmark and supplies its intended comparison function plus answer-bearing lane, emotion, and dominant-idea captions for every unit.

observations: All twelve current masters are visible at consistent panel scale beneath one much larger reference image, allowing full-body shape and broad material treatment to be compared across the cut.
The masters share centralized isolated presentation and dark backgrounds; wide nonhuman bodies are letterboxed while tall humanoids fill more of their panels.
Fine cloth, skin, bone, weapon, and paper detail is visible in the masters, but their smaller panel size reveals less detail than the enlarged reference.
defects: Unequal display scale prevents a direct full-size craft comparison between the reference and individual masters.
Intended interpretation captions sit directly beneath every master and can substitute producer description for what each image independently communicates.
uncertainties: The master images remain inspectable, but their blind semantic read is contaminated by adjacent lane, emotion, and dominant-idea text.
contamination: The master-concepts alias contains the same benchmark instructions and answer-bearing unit captions as E053.
All five global boards contain answer-bearing labels or intended-interpretation text despite the frozen blind-round exclusion of answer-bearing annotations.
The face board is especially contaminating because it supplies the exact private want, fear, strategy, emotion, contradiction, visible-face interpretation, and forbidden read beside every crop.
